Get some actual sleep before tomorrow's reading response is due at 23:30.

goals.description: Send a brief, low-effort 'goodnight' text to Roommate_R and verbally nod at Professor before heading out the door to avoid any awkwardness.
goals.rationale: I can't risk them thinking I'm abandoning the group or making things awkward with my professor. The friction of being judged right now is way worse than doing anything else. Once this tiny social exchange is done, I need to shut down immediately so I don't have to deal with the stale air or the assignment later.

goals.description: Snooze until 23:30 hits, then write the reading response in one short burst while feeling totally wiped out.
goals.rationale: Sleeping first is way more important than finishing this soon. If I drag myself out of bed early to write it, I'll be stressed and maybe forget everything tomorrow anyway. I'll tackle it when I'm tired enough to not care about the quality.

goals.description: Clean the room and change clothes tomorrow morning after I've already slept through the night.
goals.rationale: Dealing with the smell and the mess tomorrow means less friction tonight. I don't want to spend extra mental energy on chores now; if I wait until I'm alone and exhausted, I can handle it without feeling guilty about the current situation.

agent.last_reasoning: I need to finish this quick so I can sleep and avoid the smell tomorrow. The deadline is looming, but doing it now feels easier than confronting roommate_R about the odor. I'll just text a goodnight note first to keep things smooth.

goals.description: Do a quick, low-effort freshen-up of my immediate space (change out of the smelly clothes, wipe down desk surface) before heading to lunch.

But since we are close and I value avoiding friction above all else, dealing with the stale air that's distracting me and potentially making them uncomfortable is urgent. It's a short fix that protects our relationship.
